Title: Tetraose steroidal glycoalkaloids from potato provide resistance against Alternaria solani and Colorado potato beetle

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: tetraose SGA sugar-decoration step; commertetraose branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: adds a hexose (glucose) to triose steroidal glycoalkaloids to form tetraose SGAs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: triose SGAs from S. commersonii CGN18024_3 (solanidine-Gal-Glu-Glu; demissidine-Gal-Glu-Glu)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: commertetraose SGAs (Gal-Glu-Glu-Glu), including commersonine/dehydrocommersonine-type tetraose SGAs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: resistance to Alternaria solani, Colorado potato beetle, and inhibitory activity against diverse fungi
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: converts susceptible triose-SGA profile to tetraose SGAs and confers resistance to A. solani and Colorado potato beetle in S. commersonii
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: fine mapping; BSR-Seq expression evidence; transient expression in leaves; stable overexpression/transformation; UPLC-MS metabolite profiling; disease assay; insect bioassay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in resistant progeny bulks; RNA isolated from leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in transformed leaves/plants; endogenous gene absent in susceptible CGN18024_3
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in pK7WG2 (inferred from vector use)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: BSR-Seq RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA792513 (SRR17334110; SRR17334111; SRR17334112; SRR17334113); genome assembly data BioProject PRJNA789120 (SRR17348659; SRR17348660)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: A. solani inoculation; Colorado potato beetle feeding
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: SGAs can be toxic to humans and potentially affect non-target beneficial organisms; no growth penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for breeding or engineering broad-spectrum disease and pest resistance by introducing tetraose SGA biosynthesis, but deployment should restrict accumulation in edible tissues and consider toxicity/environmental risks
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ScGTR1 transformants mainly produced glucosylated tetraose SGAs but small amounts of xylosylated products were also detected; did not alter SGA profile in S. tuberosum cv. Atlantic, likely due to different triose SGA substrates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ScGTR1 was fine-mapped within the dominant resistance locus on chromosome 12 and expressed only in resistant material. 2. Stable expression in susceptible S. commersonii converted triose SGAs to tetraose SGAs by adding a glucose residue. 3. This metabolic conversion restored resistance to Alternaria solani and Colorado potato beetle.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: tetraose SGA sugar-decoration step; lycotetraose branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: adds a pentose (xylose) to triose steroidal glycoalkaloids to form tetraose SGAs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: triose SGAs from S. commersonii CGN18024_3 (solanidine-Gal-Glu-Glu; demissidine-Gal-Glu-Glu)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: lycotetraose SGAs (Gal-Glu-Glu-Xyl), including demissine/dehydrodemissine-type tetraose SGAs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: resistance to Alternaria solani, Colorado potato beetle, and inhibitory activity against diverse fungi
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: converts susceptible triose-SGA profile to tetraose SGAs and confers resistance to A. solani and Colorado potato beetle in S. commersonii
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: fine mapping; BSR-Seq expression evidence; transient expression in leaves; stable overexpression/transformation; UPLC-MS metabolite profiling; disease assay; insect bioassay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in resistant progeny bulks; RNA isolated from leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in transformed leaves/plants; endogenous gene absent in susceptible CGN18024_3
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in pK7WG2 (inferred from vector use)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: BSR-Seq RNA-seq, BioProject PRJNA792513 (SRR17334110; SRR17334111; SRR17334112; SRR17334113); genome assembly data BioProject PRJNA789120 (SRR17348659; SRR17348660)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: A. solani inoculation; Colorado potato beetle feeding
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: SGAs can be toxic to humans and potentially affect non-target beneficial organisms; no growth penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for breeding or engineering broad-spectrum disease and pest resistance by introducing tetraose SGA biosynthesis, but deployment should restrict accumulation in edible tissues and consider toxicity/environmental risks
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ScGTR2 transformants mainly produced xylosylated tetraose SGAs but small amounts of glucosylated products were also detected; did not alter SGA profile in S. tuberosum cv. Atlantic, likely due to different triose SGA substrates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ScGTR2 was fine-mapped within the dominant resistance locus on chromosome 12 and expressed only in resistant material. 2. Stable expression in susceptible S. commersonii converted triose SGAs to tetraose SGAs by adding a xylose residue. 3. This metabolic conversion restored resistance to Alternaria solani and Colorado potato beetle.
